markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose save/load/conflict/index/projection errors inline and in backend diagnostics. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: EditorBackendErrorStates leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EditorBackendErrorStates is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 53b5e903: backend_error.test.ts 6/6 + RichDocumentView conflict test - typed inline backend errors (save/conflict/schema/load/index/projection), never blank; Monaco-mount-failure textarea fallback. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

findings.title: Conflict guidance leads the operator into irrecoverable local-edit loss; dirty-flag race silently discards keystrokes typed during an in-flight save
findings.evidence: Two coupled defects on the save/error surface this MT owns: (1) backend_error.ts:44-48 conflict hint says "Reload to get the latest version, then re-apply your edit" but Reload (RichDocumentView.tsx:58-91) discards editorContent with no draft, no export, no confirmation - following the official hint destroys the unsaved work the conflict was protecting. (2) RichDocumentView.tsx:115-141 onSave captures editorContent at click time; keystrokes during the in-flight await mark isDirty=true, then the save resolution unconditionally setIsDirty(false) - the Save button disables with UNSAVED content in the editor and "Saved at" showing success. (3) No beforeunload/doc-switch guard: switching documentId or closing with dirty edits silently discards them.
findings.fix: On conflict: snapshot the local doc (e.g. localStorage/recovery table) BEFORE reload and offer re-apply; fix the race by capturing a save-generation counter (only clear dirty if no onChange occurred since capture); add an unsaved-changes guard on unmount/doc switch. Regression tests for all three.

findings.title: Error classification by substring over free-text messages misroutes errors
findings.evidence: backend_error.ts:28-51 - any save failure whose message contains "version" (e.g. a backend message like "document_versions table unavailable") classifies as conflict with the misleading reload hint; "schema" anywhere wins over conflict. The rich-doc API (api.ts request) throws plain Errors with message text, so classification is best-effort string matching.
findings.fix: Return structured error codes from the API layer (status + typed code in the JSON error body) and classify on those; keep substring matching only as fallback.

findings.title: Blocks panel renders a stale tree after save
findings.evidence: RichDocumentView.tsx:126 setLoad({...load, document: result.document, tree: load.tree}) - doc_version advances but tree.blocks remain the pre-save snapshot until manual reload; the visual-debug block list lies about current content.
findings.fix: Refresh blocks in refreshSidecars (loadRichDocumentBlocks) after save, or mark the panel as stale-until-reload.

parity_gaps: ED-DBG-002 inline backend errors: PRESENT (typed kinds + banner tested) but the conflict REMEDIATION path is data-lossy
ED-REC-001 crash/draft recovery: no editor-side draft persistence at all (descope noted on MT-159, but the conflict hint depends on it)
missing_tests: typing during an in-flight save keeps isDirty=true after the save resolves
conflict -> reload preserves (or explicitly offers) the local unsaved doc
document switch / unmount with dirty edits prompts or snapshots
misclassification guard: save error mentioning "version" in a non-conflict sense

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
